PROJECT LITRE OF LIGHT- AN INITIATIVE AIMING TOWARDS ILLUMINATING INDIA
RENEWABLY
 Project Litre of Light is a grassroots movement led by me in my school, started as an initiative to
provide affordable, sustainable lighting solutions to underprivileged communities lacking access to
electricity. The initiative was inspired by a simple yet innovative idea: transforming plastic bottles into
solar-powered light bulbs.
 The project utilizes readily available materials, primarily plastic bottles, and solar panels to create a
solar-powered light source, a powerful compact lantern.
 The materials used are low-cost and readily available, making this a cost-effective solution for
communities with limited resources. The cost of one bulb rounds off to a total of  150.
 We aim to provide solar-powered light bulbs to underdeveloped areas near Dehradun which lack easy
access to electricity. We were successful and graced enough to be able to light up the world of many
families in Thakurpur district of Dehradun and we aim to continue the good work.
COMMUNITY SERVICE
GoFundin - LETS HELP BEING THE RESPONSIBLE YOUNG ONES (2021-22)
 With the determination to raise funds to support the fight against the second wave of COVID-19, my
batchmates and I raised 4 lakh INR with this initiative.
 We inaugurated a COVID Testing Complex outside the Government Hospital, CHC Badkhalsa. Funded
by Gofundin and initiated by Dr. J.S. Punia, Chief Medical Officer, Sonipat.
 Donated medicines to underprivileged COVID patients, a water cooler outside the hospital to provide
accessible drinking water and wheelchairs to RC Memorial Hospital, Kanpur.
 Our efforts were commended by Dr. J.S. Punia for innovative and outstanding work at a young age and
were awarded certificates and promised a District Award from the Deputy Commissioner, Sonipat.
 Most importantly, I gained experience in planning, funding, and executing community health projects
which helped me demonstrate my ability to create and implement unique solutions in such alarming
situations.
DISHA SOCIAL ORGANIZATION (2022-now)
 Disha, established in 1984, aims to mobilize women, the poor, marginalized, and vulnerable sections of
society into sustainable groups for achieving social, economic, and political empowerment.
 I have been a part of Disha for the past three years, showing my unbridled desire to uplift the weaker
section of society including women and children.
 I have persuaded over 250 illiterate women in Chilkana village, Saharanpur district, to join Disha.
 I have led various workshops and training sessions for volunteers to impart knowledge about beneficial
schemes and taken initiatives to educate and mobilize communities.
 I also took lessons in digital literacy for rural girls aged 6 to 14 years and taught science to
underprivileged children for the Junior Science Program.
LEADERSHIP ROLES
School Campus And Event Management Official (2024-25)
 As a School Campus and Event Management Official, the primary responsibilities encompass the
effective maintenance and supervision of the school's physical environment as well as the organization
and execution of various school events.
 In terms of event management, the responsibilities include planning, coordinating, and overseeing a
wide range of school events, from sports meets and cultural festivals to parent-teacher meetings and
other ceremonies.
 I also help address and resolve any issues that arise promptly, ensuring smooth operations and
successful event execution.
 Overall, the role is crucial in creating a safe, efficient, and vibrant school community through
meticulous campus management and dynamic event planning.
Editorial Board of Tech Key Magazine (2024-2025)
 As a member of the editorial board of TechKey Magazine, the premier tech-focused publication at my
school, I have been responsible for overseeing the entire publication process, from content creation to
final distribution.
 My role involves curating, editing, and proofreading articles to ensure high-quality and engaging
content that aligns with the magazine's vision and standards.
 I plan and brainstorm ideas for upcoming issues, and manage deadlines to ensure timely publication.
 Overall, working in this Magazine provides a comprehensive skill set and experience that can
significantly enhance your readiness for diverse career paths and academic pursuits.
Student Developer  Banking Software Project(2021)
 Collaborated with teacher mentor Amit Mahajan to design and implement a banking software system
using Visual Basic.
 Developed core modules for account creation, transactions, and balance management, improving
understanding of software development lifecycle.
 Gained early exposure to problem-solving, debugging, and real-world applications of programming
concepts.
 Programmed features for:
1. Weekly tuck/food allowances with spending limits.
2. Fine deductions for school-related penalties.
3. Final account balance calculations at the end of each term.
4. Transaction tracking and withdrawals for student expenses.
RESEARCH PAPER
Educational programs and their dual impact on employability and long-term well-being
(https://www.mathematicaljournal.com/archives/2024.v5.i1.A.111)
 Published the research paper in the Journal of Mathematical Problems, Equations and Statistics.
 I conducted research in Adampur, Saharanpur, focusing on local educational backgrounds and their effect on career
opportunities.
 Investigated the correlation between education levels and employment outcomes using Pearsons correlation
coefficient. Analyzed the impact of education on qualitative virtues such as satisfaction and happiness through
chi-square tests.
 I identified how education influences job prospects, career satisfaction, and overall happiness.
 This provided insights into the broader benefits of education beyond immediate employment outcomes.
CERTIFICATION AND COURSES
Computational Thinking for Problem Solving  University of Pennsylvania (Coursera) (2025)
 Learned to break down complex problems into smaller computational steps using algorithms, logic, and
abstraction.
 Gained hands-on practice with Python programming basics, problem decomposition, and iterative design.
 Applied computational methods to real-world contexts, reinforcing skills essential for Data Science and software
development.
 Strengthened ability to translate problems from diverse domains into computational solutions.
